revision.comment: 「[[鹿江比賣神社]]」を「[[葦稲葉神社]]」へ移動: 異論なきため
revision.text: #転送 [[葦稲葉神社]]

revision.comment: 移動後の改稿
revision.text:
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社|神社（式内社）]]。
『延喜式神名帳』[[阿波国]][[板野郡]]に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、[[元慶]]7年（[[883年]]）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『[[日本三代実録]]』）、現在その[[論社]]として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から[[草野姫|草野姫（かやのひめ）]]と同神とされ（『[[古事記]]』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『[[日本書紀]]』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、[[草]]の神とされている。
*[[上板町|徳島県板野郡上板町]]に鎮座する[[葦稲葉神社]]に[[合祀]]されている鹿江比売命 *[[鳴門市|徳島県鳴門市]]に鎮座する[[大麻比古神社]]の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]]

『延喜式神名帳』[[阿波国]][[板野郡]]に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、[[元慶]]7年（[[883年]]）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『[[日本三代実録]]』）、現在その[[論社]]として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から[[草野姫|草野姫（かやのひめ）]]と同神とされ（『[[古事記]]』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『[[日本書紀]]』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、[[草]]の神とされている。
*[[上板町|徳島県板野郡上板町]]に鎮座する[[葦稲葉神社]]に[[合祀]]されている鹿江比売命 *[[鳴門市|徳島県鳴門市]]に鎮座する[[大麻比古神社]]の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}} [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

revision.comment: 修正
revision.text:
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社|神社（式内社）]]。
== 概要 == 『延喜式神名帳』[[阿波国]][[板野郡]]に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、[[元慶]]7年（[[883年]]）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『[[日本三代実録]]』）、現在その[[論社]]として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から[[草野姫|草野姫（かやのひめ）]]と同神とされ（『[[古事記]]』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『[[日本書紀]]』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、[[草]]の神とされているが、実際には[[大麻比古命]]の子・[[由布津主命]]の妹・[[千鹿江比売命]]であり、阿波忌部一族の神である。
*[[上板町|徳島県板野郡上板町]]に鎮座する[[葦稲葉神社]]に[[合祀]]されている鹿江比売命 *[[鳴門市|徳島県鳴門市]]に鎮座する[[大麻比古神社]]の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}} [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

== 概要 == 『延喜式神名帳』[[阿波国]][[板野郡]]に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、[[元慶]]7年（[[883年]]）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『[[日本三代実録]]』）、現在その[[論社]]として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から[[草野姫|草野姫（かやのひめ）]]と同神とされ（『[[古事記]]』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『[[日本書紀]]』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、[[草]]の神とされているが、実際には[[大麻比古命]]の子・[[由布津主命]]の妹・[[千鹿江比売命]]であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
*[[上板町|徳島県板野郡上板町]]に鎮座する[[葦稲葉神社]]に[[合祀]]されている鹿江比売命 *[[鳴門市|徳島県鳴門市]]に鎮座する[[大麻比古神社]]の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Kaehime Shrine

| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported
revision.text:
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]] [[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]] '''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社|神社（式内社）]]。
== 概要 == 『延喜式神名帳』[[阿波国]][[板野郡]]に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、[[元慶]]7年（[[883年]]）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『[[日本三代実録]]』）、現在その[[論社]]として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から[[草野姫|草野姫（かやのひめ）]]と同神とされ（『[[古事記]]』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『[[日本書紀]]』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、[[草]]の神とされているが、実際には[[大麻比古命]]の子・[[由布津主命]]の妹・[[千鹿江比売命]]であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
*[[上板町|徳島県板野郡上板町]]に鎮座する[[葦稲葉神社]]に[[合祀]]されている鹿江比売命 *[[鳴門市|徳島県鳴門市]]に鎮座する[[大麻比古神社]]の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}} [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

*[[上板町|徳島県板野郡上板町]]に鎮座する[[葦稲葉神社]]に[[合祀]]されている鹿江比売命 *[[鳴門市|徳島県鳴門市]]に鎮座する[[大麻比古神社]]の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q11677210}} [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|鹿江比賣神社|version=101896883|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == [[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]] [[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]] '''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社|神社（式内社）]]。
== 概要 == 『延喜式神名帳』[[阿波国]][[板野郡]]に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、[[元慶]]7年（[[883年]]）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『[[日本三代実録]]』）、現在その[[論社]]として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から[[草野姫|草野姫（かやのひめ）]]と同神とされ（『[[古事記]]』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『[[日本書紀]]』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、[[草]]の神とされているが、実際には[[大麻比古命]]の子・[[由布津主命]]の妹・[[千鹿江比売命]]であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
*[[上板町|徳島県板野郡上板町]]に鎮座する[[葦稲葉神社]]に[[合祀]]されている鹿江比売命 *[[鳴門市|徳島県鳴門市]]に鎮座する[[大麻比古神社]]の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年
[[Category:阿波国の式内社]]
==End Japanese==

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q11677210}} [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|鹿江比賣神社|version=101896883|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == [[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]] [[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]] '''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社|神社（式内社）]]。
== 概要 == 『延喜式神名帳』[[阿波国]][[板野郡]]に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、[[元慶]]7年（[[883年]]）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『[[日本三代実録]]』）、現在その[[論社]]として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から[[草野姫|草野姫（かやのひめ）]]と同神とされ（『[[古事記]]』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『[[日本書紀]]』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、[[草]]の神とされているが、実際には[[大麻比古命]]の子・[[由布津主命]]の妹・[[千鹿江比売命]]であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
*[[上板町|徳島県板野郡上板町]]に鎮座する[[葦稲葉神社]]に[[合祀]]されている鹿江比売命 *[[鳴門市|徳島県鳴門市]]に鎮座する[[大麻比古神社]]の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年
==End Japanese== [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

== 概要 == 『延喜式神名帳』{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}{{jalink|板野郡|Q1156025}}に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）、現在その{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から{{jalink|草野姫||草野姫（かやのひめ）}}と同神とされ（『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、{{jalink|草|Q11618055}}の神とされているが、実際には{{jalink|大麻比古命}}の子・{{jalink|由布津主命}}の妹・{{jalink|千鹿江比売命}}であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。

== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== street address (P6375) ==

* {'text': '徳島県板野郡上板町神宅', 'language': 'ja'}






[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]]
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|式内社||神社（式内社）}}。

=== 概要 ===
『延喜式神名帳』{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}{{jalink|板野郡|Q1156025}}に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）、現在その{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から{{jalink|草野姫||草野姫（かやのひめ）}}と同神とされ（『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、{{jalink|草|Q11618055}}の神とされているが、実際には{{jalink|大麻比古命}}の子・{{jalink|由布津主命}}の妹・{{jalink|千鹿江比売命}}であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。

*{{jalink|上板町|Q1350072|徳島県板野郡上板町}}に鎮座する{{jalink|葦稲葉神社|Q11621139}}に{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}されている鹿江比売命
*{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812|徳島県鳴門市}}に鎮座する{{jalink|大麻比古神社|Q723417}}の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年

{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]]
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|式内社||神社（式内社）}}。

=== 概要 ===
『延喜式神名帳』{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}{{jalink|板野郡|Q1156025}}に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）、現在その{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から{{jalink|草野姫||草野姫（かやのひめ）}}と同神とされ（『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、{{jalink|草|Q11618055}}の神とされているが、実際には{{jalink|大麻比古命}}の子・{{jalink|由布津主命}}の妹・{{jalink|千鹿江比売命}}であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。

*{{jalink|上板町|Q1350072|徳島県板野郡上板町}}に鎮座する{{jalink|葦稲葉神社|Q11621139}}に{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}されている鹿江比売命
*{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812|徳島県鳴門市}}に鎮座する{{jalink|大麻比古神社|Q723417}}の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年

{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== street address (P6375) ==

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]]
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|式内社||神社（式内社）}}。

=== 概要 ===
『延喜式神名帳』{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}{{jalink|板野郡|Q1156025}}に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）、現在その{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から{{jalink|草野姫||草野姫（かやのひめ）}}と同神とされ（『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、{{jalink|草|Q11618055}}の神とされているが、実際には{{jalink|大麻比古命}}の子・{{jalink|由布津主命}}の妹・{{jalink|千鹿江比売命}}であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。

*{{jalink|上板町|Q1350072|徳島県板野郡上板町}}に鎮座する{{jalink|葦稲葉神社|Q11621139}}に{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}されている鹿江比売命
*{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812|徳島県鳴門市}}に鎮座する{{jalink|大麻比古神社|Q723417}}の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年

{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]]
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|式内社||神社（式内社）}}。

=== 概要 ===
『延喜式神名帳』{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}{{jalink|板野郡|Q1156025}}に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）、現在その{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から{{jalink|草野姫||草野姫（かやのひめ）}}と同神とされ（『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、{{jalink|草|Q11618055}}の神とされているが、実際には{{jalink|大麻比古命}}の子・{{jalink|由布津主命}}の妹・{{jalink|千鹿江比売命}}であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。

*{{jalink|上板町|Q1350072|徳島県板野郡上板町}}に鎮座する{{jalink|葦稲葉神社|Q11621139}}に{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}されている鹿江比売命
*{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812|徳島県鳴門市}}に鎮座する{{jalink|大麻比古神社|Q723417}}の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== street address (P6375) ==

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]]
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|式内社||神社（式内社）}}。

=== 概要 ===
『延喜式神名帳』{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}{{jalink|板野郡|Q1156025}}に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）、現在その{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から{{jalink|草野姫||草野姫（かやのひめ）}}と同神とされ（『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、{{jalink|草|Q11618055}}の神とされているが、実際には{{jalink|大麻比古命}}の子・{{jalink|由布津主命}}の妹・{{jalink|千鹿江比売命}}であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。

*{{jalink|上板町|Q1350072|徳島県板野郡上板町}}に鎮座する{{jalink|葦稲葉神社|Q11621139}}に{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}されている鹿江比売命
*{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812|徳島県鳴門市}}に鎮座する{{jalink|大麻比古神社|Q723417}}の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年

{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]]
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|式内社||神社（式内社）}}。

=== 概要 ===
『延喜式神名帳』{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}{{jalink|板野郡|Q1156025}}に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）、現在その{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から{{jalink|草野姫||草野姫（かやのひめ）}}と同神とされ（『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、{{jalink|草|Q11618055}}の神とされているが、実際には{{jalink|大麻比古命}}の子・{{jalink|由布津主命}}の妹・{{jalink|千鹿江比売命}}であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。

*{{jalink|上板町|Q1350072|徳島県板野郡上板町}}に鎮座する{{jalink|葦稲葉神社|Q11621139}}に{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}されている鹿江比売命
*{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812|徳島県鳴門市}}に鎮座する{{jalink|大麻比古神社|Q723417}}の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年

{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== street address (P6375) ==

* {'text': '徳島県板野郡上板町神宅', 'language': 'ja'}






[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|上板町神宅にある神社。]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）にある造営記念碑。]]
'''鹿江比賣神社'''（'''鹿江比売神社'''）（かえひめじんじゃ）は『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|式内社||神社（式内社）}}。

=== 概要 ===
『延喜式神名帳』{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}{{jalink|板野郡|Q1156025}}に小社（国幣小社）として掲載され、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月5日には従五位上に叙された記録があるが（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）、現在その{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}として以下の2社が挙げられている。なお、祭神は鹿江比売神（命）と見られており、「かえひめ」の訓から{{jalink|草野姫||草野姫（かやのひめ）}}と同神とされ（『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』には「鹿屋野比売神」、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には「草祖（くさのおや）草野姫」として現れる）、{{jalink|草|Q11618055}}の神とされているが、実際には{{jalink|大麻比古命}}の子・{{jalink|由布津主命}}の妹・{{jalink|千鹿江比売命}}であり、阿波忌部一族の神である{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。

*{{jalink|上板町|Q1350072|徳島県板野郡上板町}}に鎮座する{{jalink|葦稲葉神社|Q11621139}}に{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}されている鹿江比売命
*{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812|徳島県鳴門市}}に鎮座する{{jalink|大麻比古神社|Q723417}}の境内にある小祠、鹿江比売神社

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第23巻南海道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和62年

{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine (Shikinaisha)||shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} {{jalink|Itano District|Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{jalink|Kayanohime||Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{jalink|grass|Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{jalink|Ōasahiko no Mikoto}} and younger sister of {{jalink|Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{jalink|Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139}}, which is {{jalink|merged shrine|Q11412524}} in {{jalink|Kamiita Town|Q1350072|Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{jalink|Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417}} located in {{jalink|Naruto|Q460812|Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine (Shikinaisha)||shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} {{jalink|Itano District|Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{jalink|Kayanohime||Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{jalink|grass|Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{jalink|Ōasahiko no Mikoto}} and younger sister of {{jalink|Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{jalink|Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139}}, which is {{jalink|merged shrine|Q11412524}} in {{jalink|Kamiita Town|Q1350072|Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{jalink|Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417}} located in {{jalink|Naruto|Q460812|Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine (Shikinaisha)||shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} {{jalink|Itano District|Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{jalink|Kayanohime||Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{jalink|grass|Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{jalink|Ōasahiko no Mikoto}} and younger sister of {{jalink|Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{jalink|Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139}}, which is {{jalink|merged shrine|Q11412524}} in {{jalink|Kamiita Town|Q1350072|Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{jalink|Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417}} located in {{jalink|Naruto|Q460812|Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine (Shikinaisha)||shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} {{jalink|Itano District|Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{jalink|Kayanohime||Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{jalink|grass|Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{jalink|Ōasahiko no Mikoto}} and younger sister of {{jalink|Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{jalink|Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139}}, which is {{jalink|merged shrine|Q11412524}} in {{jalink|Kamiita Town|Q1350072|Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{jalink|Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417}} located in {{jalink|Naruto|Q460812|Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine (Shikinaisha)||shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} {{jalink|Itano District|Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{jalink|Kayanohime||Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{jalink|grass|Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{jalink|Ōasahiko no Mikoto}} and younger sister of {{jalink|Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{jalink|Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139}}, which is {{jalink|merged shrine|Q11412524}} in {{jalink|Kamiita Town|Q1350072|Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{jalink|Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417}} located in {{jalink|Naruto|Q460812|Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine (Shikinaisha)||shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} {{jalink|Itano District|Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{jalink|Kayanohime||Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{jalink|grass|Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{jalink|Ōasahiko no Mikoto}} and younger sister of {{jalink|Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{jalink|Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139}}, which is {{jalink|merged shrine|Q11412524}} in {{jalink|Kamiita Town|Q1350072|Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{jalink|Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417}} located in {{jalink|Naruto|Q460812|Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)||WD=|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime||WD=|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill||lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill||lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)||WD=|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime||WD=|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)||WD=|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime||WD=|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{DEFAULTSORT:かえひめしんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

{{nihongo|'''Kaehime Shrine'''|鹿江比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:鹿江比賣神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11677210}}

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|WD=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|WD=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|WD=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|WD=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
* ''Shikinaisha Investigation Report'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987 (Shōwa 62)

== Japanese content ==
[[ファイル:鹿江比売神社.jpg|サムネイル|A shrine located in Kamitaku, Kamiita Town.]]
[[ファイル:葦稲葉神社（鹿江比売神社）の石碑.jpg|サムネイル|Commemorative monument at Ashiinaba Shrine (Kaehime Shrine).]]
'''Kaehime Shrine''' (also written '''Kaehime Shrine''') (かえひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine (Shikinaisha)}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''.

=== Overview ===
In the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', under {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|qid=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|qid=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}, it is listed as a small shrine (kokuhei shōsha). There is a record that on September 5, 883 (Genkei 7), it was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). At present, the following two shrines are considered candidate {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. The enshrined deity is thought to be Kaehime no Kami (no Mikoto), and from the reading "kaehime" it is considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayanohime}} (appearing as "Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and as "Kusa no Oya Kayanohime" in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}''). She is treated as a deity of {{ill|lt=grass|Q11618055|qid=Q11618055|1=grass}}, but in fact is Chikakahi-me no Mikoto, child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōasahiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and younger sister of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yufutsu-nushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, a deity of the Awa Imbe lineage{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

* Kaehime no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Ashiinaba Shrine|Q11621139|qid=Q11621139|1=Ashiinaba Shrine}}, which is {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiita Town|Q1350072|qid=Q1350072|lt=Kamiita Town, Itano District, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Kamiita, Tokushima}}
* Kaehime Shrine, a small shrine within {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|qid=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Naruto|Q460812|qid=Q460812|lt=Naruto City, Tokushima Prefecture|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}

=== References ===
